name: Zucchini-And-Basil Filo
recipeIngredient: 1 small Red onion, diced into 1/4-inch pieces
8 tablespoons Unsalted butter, melted (or a mixture of butter and olive oil)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Remove the filo pastry from the freezer and let it come to room temperature while you prepare the filling.
3. Unfold the dough and cut the stack of sheets in two. If making just one recipe, refold half the dough and wrap it in plastic; it can be kept for a few days in the refrigerator or refrozen.
4. Grate the zucchini on the large holes of a hand grater or food processor. Toss with salt and set aside for 30 minutes. Then drain and squeeze dry in a clean kitchen towel.
5. Roast the pine nuts for 5-to-8 minutes in the oven, chop them finely, and set aside.
6. Heat olive oil in a large skillet and sauté the onion until it begins to soften, about 2 minutes. Add the zucchini, season with freshly ground black pepper, and cook another 4 minutes. Then add garlic, basil, parsley, and white wine or water. Cover for 3 to 4 minutes and remove from heat.
7. Beat the eggs, stir in the cheeses, then cook the vegetables. Check the seasoning.
8. Brush a 9-by-13-by-2-inch pan with melted butter. Lay a sheet of filo pastry with butter (or a mixture of butter and olive oil) on top. Continue brushing and layering until half the sheets are used.
9. Scatter half of the chopped pine nuts between several layers. Brush the top layer with butter and spread the filling over it.
10. Continue layering the rest of the pastry sheets. If the butter begins to congeal, reheat it so it spreads easily.
11. Cut the assembled pastry into 3-inch squares, then into diamonds, making sure you cut through all layers. Refrigerate the pastry if not baking immediately.
12. Bake in a preheated oven at 400°F for 40 to 50 minutes or until browned. Serve the pastries warm from the oven, slightly cooled, or at room temperature. Makes one 9-by-13-inch filo pastry.
